title: cuscuta planiflora var. ambigua
intro: Cuscuta planiflora var. ambigua is a plant species also known as Chinese dodder, which is a type of parasitic vine that grows by attaching itself to other plants and obtaining nutrients through a network of fine threads. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and asthma symptoms, and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta planiflora var. ambigua is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also dried and used as a powder or capsule supplement.
